sprint: Frame Variation Seeding Now Re-Seeds Correctly on Every Representation Switch
task: A second real, user-reported bug from the same authoring pass: 'when i authored in builder i gave white and red borders to each frame ... it did not come to the creator.' Reproduced the exact scenario: two separate single-Place Scenes/Representations, each with its own distinct Frame (white / red), switched between via Change Look. js/creationFlow.js's _seedDefaultFrameVariation() writes a Representation's authored defaultFrame onto the page's artwork override bag -- but only the FIRST time, guarded by a check that can't tell an auto-seeded value apart from a genuine Story Author pick in Card Designer. Once the FIRST Representation ever seen had seeded its own Frame, every LATER Representation's own distinct Frame became permanently unreachable -- the border stayed stuck on whichever Scene was seen first. Fixed by tracking provenance: a companion _seededFrameVariation field records what this function itself last wrote, and re-seeding is refused only when the current value has drifted from that recorded value (meaning a real manual pick happened in between). Verified via two new Playwright tests: the exact reported white/red sequence confirmed broken before the fix and fixed after; a three-Representation white/red/blue sequence with each switch's border colour pixel-sampled and confirmed correct, proving re-seeding isn't a one-shot fix; and a direct check confirming a genuine manual Frame Variation pick still survives a later switch untouched. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (24 scripts) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
